author: @benjaminn8
summary: --query flag now wired into GraphQL variables on 5 list commands, including the matching $query argument declarations in queries.go.
reason: Generated template emits a --query flag on orders/customers/products/inventory-items/abandoned-checkouts list commands but (a) the Go side never passes the value into the GraphQL variables map and (b) the corresponding GraphQL queries in internal/client/queries.go don't declare a $query: String variable or pass it into the connection's `query:` argument. Either half alone is a silent no-op. Fix is two-part: add the conditional variables["query"] = flagQuery bind in each list command, and add `$query: String` to the operation signature plus `query: $query` to the connection in queries.go. Fulfillment-orders list is intentionally left dead because Shopify's fulfillmentOrders GraphQL field has no query arg; that is API truth, not a template bug. Generator template bug filed as cli-printing-press#2058.
validated_outcome: shopify-pp-cli orders list --query 'created_at:>2026-05-01' --first 5 returns only orders created after 2026-05-01 against a live store; without the fix the filter silently no-ops and returns the oldest 5 orders in the store.
